description: Documentation of missing data patterns and handling strategies.
Description of raw data sources before preprocessing.
Details about informed consent procedures, including consent type, documentation, and withdrawal mechanisms.
Description of the larger set from which the sample was drawn, if any.
Description of the sampling strategy (deterministic, probabilistic, etc.).
Description of patterns in missing data (e.g., missing completely at random, missing at random, missing not at random).
Detailed description of where raw data comes from (e.g., sensors, databases, web APIs, manual collection).
Information on how to access or retrieve the raw source data.
Additional details on annotation quality assessment and findings.
Descriptions of what each tool does in the annotation process and what types of annotations it produces. Should correspond to the tools list.
Notes or caveats about using the dataset for intended purposes.
What ethics review board(s) reviewed this research? Include institution names and approval details.
How is consent documented? Include references to consent forms or procedures used.
What methods were used to anonymize or de-identify participant data? Include technical details of privacy-preserving techniques.
Description of how this variable was derived or calculated from other variables, if applicable.
Notes about data quality, reliability, or known issues specific to this variable.
